key_insight: CWGD reweights gradient noise by local curvature, giving a more faithful measure of optimization noise than plain variance and enabling better learning-rate scheduling.
engineering_relevance: For SGD-based training, this can improve convergence efficiency and final error with little overhead, especially when curvature varies strongly across parameter directions.
method: Compute per-sample gradient diversity with an inverse-square-root Hessian weighting.
Use a Hutchinson-based diagonal Hessian estimator, which is exact on quadratic objectives.
Modulate cosine learning-rate decay using the CWGD signal to adapt to geometry.
evidence_signal: On strongly convex quadratics across different condition numbers, batch sizes, and noise structures, CWGD-Cosine achieved about 20% lower final optimization error than standard cosine annealing, with up to a 2x reduction in asymptotic error floor in the analyzed setting.
watch_item: The approach depends on Hessian estimates that can become stale in non-convex training, which may limit reliability outside the quadratic/convex regime.
paper_title: Curvature-Weighted Gradient Diversity: A Noise Measure for Geometry-Adaptive SGD Schedules
